content.full_text:
Letter from SMUD to Scott Hallabrin

March 19, 1992

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

The SMUD Legal Department is requesting an advice letter from the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding whether or not a series of information letters and videotapes produced at SMUD's expense would be in violation of FPPC Regulation §18901 and Government Code §89001 and/or §82041.5. SMUD legal staff spoke to FPPC staff attorney Blanca Breeze about this issue on March 10 and 11, 1992.

SMUD plans to mail information letters to the presidents of community groups at their business or residential addresses informing them about the availability of SMUD speaker presentations. The information letter will not mention any elected official by name or by office; nor will the letter be signed by any elected official. At the specific request of a community group, SMUD staff will provide a program either on site at SMUD or in a community setting. There may be more than 200 of these letters per calendar month mailed to the public.

SMUD speakers will use a series of videotapes depicting various topics related to the provision of electrical service including but not limited to production, generation, cogeneration, transmission, conservation, and electric service rates. To present outside objective opinions regarding SMUD programs, staff would like to single out elected officials who attend SMUD functions and use their quotes in the videotapes. The producers plan to introduce the President of the SMUD Board of Directors by name who will speak a couple of sentences regarding SMUD policy. In addition, this particular videotape singles out Congressman Vic Fazio who will also provide a brief quote in support of SMUD programs.

Scott Hallabrin

March 19, 1992

SMUD estimates that less than 200 of these presentations will be given in response to customer requests in a calendar month.

Should you or your staff have any questions, please feel free to call me at 732-6126.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Dana S. Appling
Senior Attorney

cc: Jan Schori
Monica Siewert
